description:
Legal document (ḥujja) issued by the chief qadi court of Ottoman Cairo, al-Bāb al-ʿĀlī. Non-Geniza. Dated 19 Jumādā II 1051 AH (1641 CE). The document details the partition and usage agreement between shareholders in a three-story residential building on Darb al-Qadīb in the Jewish quarter. The residents are: Yaʿqūb b. Khiḍr b. Elīya, al-Yahūdī al-Rabbān (12 shares); Yaḥya b. Ibrahīm b. Yaḥya, al-Yahūdī al-Maghribī (6 shares); Sitt al-Bayt bt. Mūsa b. Mordekhay (6 shares). 

Two of the filing notes in Judaeo-Arabic refer to the street as Darb Qadīm, which was more likely an alternate title rather than a misspelling of قضيب v.s. قديم. One of the filing notes refers to the property as a synagogue (כניס), probably from when it served as a communal house of worship at a later date, though, it's possible the designation refers to an earlier phase of usage before the ḥujja was issued. A marginal note in Arabic on the recto (ثلاث نسخ) states that "three copies" were issue: presumably to Yaʿqūb, Yaḥya, and Sitt al-Bayt. 

For document images and information on provenance, see the 2009 NLI catalogue entry by Ephraim Wust: https://www.nli.org.il/ar/manuscripts/NNL_ALEPH990029250640205171/NLI#$FL188116337

NLI Ms. Ar. 276.20 corresponds to images 39-40 at the link above.
